restaurants.ambience: A historic, elegant Belle Époque tearoom founded in 1903, known for its refined décor by Édouard-Jean Niermans and storied past—Patrons have included Marcel Proust and Coco Chanel

restaurants.name: Pink Mamma
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf
restaurants.ambience: A standout, Instagram-worthy four-story trattoria. The exterior is a pink building draped in lush greenery. Inside, each floor brings a distinct ambience—from retro bar and art-filled staircases to Tuscan-style kitchens and a photogenic rooftop with skylights and greenhouse vibes. There's even a hidden speakeasy bar in the basement

restaurants.name: Le Poulbot
restaurants.ambience: A cozy, intimate Montmartre bistro nestled just off Rue Norvins. The space is small—warm, classic Parisian charm with a low-key, unpretentious vibe
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Le Petit Capucci
restaurants.ambience: A relaxed Italian spot just a stone's throw from Galeries Lafayette. Cozy and approachable, with an open kitchen and communal seating that feels welcoming and casual
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Panasia
restaurants.ambience: A modern Asian fusion bistro inside the department store—stylish, contemporary, and convenient for a flavorful escape
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Chez Delphine
restaurants.ambience: A cozy, away-from-the-crowds bistro just a short walk from Galeries Lafayette, frequently praised by locals for its authenticity and unpretentious charm. It offers a welcoming atmosphere—with simple décor and heartfelt hospitality
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Bistrot Paul Chêne
restaurants.ambience: A charming, neighborhood-style French bistro tucked near Trocadéro—unpretentious and warmly local
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Le Grand Pan
restaurants.ambience: A highly regarded small French bistro near Trocadéro—valued for its genuine atmosphere and delicious, reasonably priced cooking
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

restaurants.name: Mokus l'Écureuil
restaurants.ambience: A warm, friendly neighborhood Italian restaurant within walking distance of the Eiffel Tower and Trocadéro. It's praised for its cozy vibe and relaxed dining experience—perfect for a satisfying dinner without pretension
must_try_dishes: freshly made pasta with seasonal vegetables
source_files: France/All Inclusive Guide- Paris, Switzerland and Italy.pdf

local_dishes.description: Traditionally topped with melted cheese and baguette croutons

local_dishes.description: A savory tart with seasonal vegetables, cream, and cheese

local_dishes.description: Simple yet iconic French roast chicken, often served with herbed potatoes

local_dishes.name: Moules Marinières
local_dishes.description: Mussels steamed in white wine, garlic, parsley, and shallots; often served with fries

local_dishes.name: Bouillabaisse (Parisian Style)
local_dishes.description: Traditional fish stew from Marseille, but many Paris brasseries serve it with fresh local seafood
